why_it_matters: HBTA is the acidic beta-diketone extractant; without pure-component and association parameters, a predictive ePC-SAFT organic phase cannot be claimed.
near_term_action: Search ePC-SAFT/PC-SAFT literature and local package tables; if absent, define a documented beta-diketone surrogate or fit pseudo-parameters.

why_it_matters: TOPO is the neutral synergist coordinating lithium; it affects organic-phase nonideality and complex stabilization.
near_term_action: Search package tables and literature; consider TOP/TBP only as documented surrogate if chemically justified.

why_it_matters: The diluent controls organic phase properties, phase split, and ion transfer; sulfonated kerosene is not a single pure component.
near_term_action: Choose a defensible pseudo-component, kerosene surrogate, n-dodecane, n-cetane, hexane, or fitted mixture surrogate with a limitation note.

why_it_matters: Lithium transfer into the organic phase likely depends on a Li-BTA-TOPO complex or equivalent association representation.
near_term_action: Define the minimum complex species or association-site representation after reaction network selection.

why_it_matters: Ca and Mg strongly inhibit or compete; ignoring them can overstate selectivity unless pretreatment is explicit.
near_term_action: Model pretreatment first; add Mg/Ca complexes only after Li/Na smoke tests are stable.

why_it_matters: Saponification/deprotonation controls the active ligand form and sodium exchange.
near_term_action: Use Shan/Zhang operating conditions first; search for equilibrium constants, otherwise fit or document stoichiometric wrapper.

why_it_matters: The Li/Na stage model now has source-regressed constants for presentation and PrOMMiS handoff, but a validated equilibrium constant is still needed to claim full multication reactive HBTA/TOPO ePC-SAFT.
near_term_action: Use hbta_topo_reactive_fit.json plus residual/uncertainty artifacts for the current case-study model; continue searching or expand the regression set with independent data.

why_it_matters: Competing-ion constants determine pretreatment severity and whether the model predicts selectivity under real brine chemistry.
near_term_action: Treat Ca/Mg removal as explicit pretreatment until constants and parameters are defensible.

why_it_matters: The source-location story needs a full Smackover feed row; current local clean USGS data provide major ions but extraction chemistry remains Shan/Gando.
near_term_action: Use the local USGS clean rows for source sensitivity; keep Shan/Gando as chemistry evidence until a matching extraction study exists.
